Items.Title: 测试小部件在 Flutter 中的外观 | Testing the widget’s appearance in Flutter
Items.Description:
一个好的程序员编写测试来发现错误并维护代码。 测试是程序员日常工作的一部分，但许多程序员忽略了一种重要的测试，即用户界面测试。 您能想到要确保小部件的外观或不为具有数千个小部件的项目引入错误有多难吗？ 如果不进行测试，这很难甚至不可能维护。 然而，Flutter Golden Test提供了一种简单的方法来帮助我们确保小部件具有正确的外观。

在本文中，您将了解如何使用Alchemist工具改进用户界面测试体验以及Golden Test的任何概念。

可维护的代码是可测试的代码。 什么是黄金测试？

记住一些单元测试的概念， Golden Test提供了一种使用称为Golden File的主图像测试单个 widget 外观的方法，该主图像被认为是 widget 的正确呈现。

设置炼金术士包

工具配置非常简单易行。 让我们开始使用以下命令将Alchemist包安装到项目中：

 flutter pub add alchemist --dev

安装Alchemist工具成功后，在test/目录下创建一个名为flutter_test_config.dart的文件，用于在执行测试前准备一些配置。 在这个文件中，我们将准备一些配置，如渲染小部件阴影，并允许显示原始文本而不是隐藏它们。

 import 'dart:async'; import 'package:alchemist/alchemist.dart'; Future<void> testExecutable(FutureOr<void> Function() testMain) async { return prepareGoldenTests(testMain); } void prepareGoldenTests(FutureOr<void> Function() testMain) { return AlchemistConfig.runWithConfig( config: const AlchemistConfig( platformGoldensConfig: PlatformGoldensConfig( renderShadows: true, obscureText: false, ), ), run: testMain, ); }

编写黄金测试

在开始编写Golden Test之前，让我们编写一个小部件进行测试。 我们为此部分创建了一个小部件，显示 NBA 比赛时间表。

 import 'package:flutter/material.dart'; class GameItem extends StatelessWidget { const GameItem({super.key}); @override Widget build(BuildContext context) { return Container( padding: const EdgeInsets.symmetric( vertical: 15, horizontal: 20, ), decoration: BoxDecoration( color: Colors.white, borderRadius: BorderRadius.circular(10), ), child: Row( crossAxisAlignment: CrossAxisAlignment.start, children: [ Expanded( child: Row( children: [ Expanded( child: Column( crossAxisAlignment: CrossAxisAlignment.start, children: const [ Text( 'Boston Celtics', style: TextStyle( fontSize: 18, fontWeight: FontWeight.w400, ), ), SizedBox(height: 15), Text( 'Chicago Bulls', style: TextStyle( fontSize: 18, fontWeight: FontWeight.w400, ), ), ], ), ), const Expanded( child: Center( child: Text( '08:00 PM', style: TextStyle( fontSize: 18, fontWeight: FontWeight.w600, ), ), ), ), ], ), ), ], ), ); } }

创建一个 testGoldens 测试

有了 widget 来测试外观，写测试就变得容易了。 使用alchemist包中的goldenTest()函数定义一个测试，此函数允许您定义一个小部件来测试外观和文件名以生成Golden File 。

 goldenTest( 'displayed the basketball game', fileName: 'baskteball_game_widget', constraints: const BoxConstraints( maxWidth: 300, ), builder: () => const GameItem(), );

生成黄金文件

在使用goldenTest()函数编写测试后，是时候生成黄金文件了。 您可以执行下面的命令来生成Golden File 。

 flutter test --update-goldens

您可能会注意到test目录已更新为一个名为goldens的新目录，其中包含由Golden Test生成的Golden File ，例如：

 test/ goldens/ ci/ - baskteball_game_widget.png macos/ - baskteball_game_widget.png

黄金测试会生成macos/子目录，因为在撰写本文时我使用的是MacOS操作系统。 无论您使用的是Linux还是Windows，您的黄金测试都会生成处理您的操作系统的子目录，这些子目录在平台之间存在细微差别。

持续集成工作流中的黄金测试

您可能会注意到goldens/目录也有ci/子目录。 生成ci/子目录是为了处理MacOS 、 Linux和Windows平台之间渲染的细微差别。

使用 —dart-define 启用持续集成

要启用持续集成环境，必须在本地禁用测试。 我们可以使用IS_CONTINUOUS_INTEGRATION环境声明来识别测试何时在持续集成环境中运行，并使用bool.fromEnvironment获取此值。

返回test/目录中的flutter_test_config.dart并更新prepareGoldenTests以获取IS_CONTINUOUS_INTEGRATION声明，并在该值为true时在本地禁用测试。

 void prepareGoldenTests(FutureOr<void> Function() testMain) { const isContinuosIntegration = bool.fromEnvironment('IS_CONTINUOUS_INTEGRATION'); return AlchemistConfig.runWithConfig( config: const AlchemistConfig( platformGoldensConfig: PlatformGoldensConfig( renderShadows: true, obscureText: false, enabled: isContinuosIntegration ), ), run: testMain, ); }

使用—dart-define运行测试

使用—-dart-define环境声明运行测试非常简单。 请参阅下面的命令以运行持续集成环境的测试。

 flutter test --dart-define=IS_CONTINUOUS_INTEGRATION=true

结论

黄金测试对于维护代码和项目的健康很重要。 有时，标准的黄金测试工具不足以涵盖所有可能的情况； 因此，使用Alchemist工具为开发人员提供了一个强大的工具来开发外观测试，并提供了一种在持续集成环境中使用它的方法。



原文:

A good programmer writes tests to find errors and maintain the code. Tests are part of a programmer guy daily, but many programmers ignore an important kind of test, the user interface test. Can you think how hard to ensure the widget's appearance or don’t introduce a bug for a project with thousands of widgets? That’s much hard or maybe impossible to maintain this without tests. However, The Flutter Golden Test provides an easy way to help us ensure that widgets have the correct appearance.

In this article, you'll learn about improving your user interface testing experience with the Alchemist tool and any concepts of the Golden Test.

The maintainable code is a testable code. - Unknown

What’s a Golden Test?

Remembering a little of any concepts of unit tests, the Golden Test provides a way to test the appearance of one single widget using a master image called Golden File which is considered the correct rendering of a widget.

Setting up the Alchemist package

The tool configuration is pretty simple and easy. Let’s start installing the Alchemist package into the project with the command below:

flutter pub add alchemist --dev

Create a file to prepare some configurations before a test is executed called flutter_test_config.dart in the test/ directory after installing the Alchemist tool successfully. In this file, we’ll prepare some configurations like render widget shadows and allow the display of raw text rather than obscure them.

import 'dart:async'; import 'package:alchemist/alchemist.dart'; Future<void> testExecutable(FutureOr<void> Function() testMain) async { return prepareGoldenTests(testMain);
} void prepareGoldenTests(FutureOr<void> Function() testMain) { return AlchemistConfig.runWithConfig( config: const AlchemistConfig( platformGoldensConfig: PlatformGoldensConfig( renderShadows: true, obscureText: false, ), ), run: testMain, );
}

Writing a Golden Test

Let’s write one widget to test before we start to write the Golden Test. We created a widget for this section displaying an NBA game schedule.

import 'package:flutter/material.dart'; class GameItem extends StatelessWidget { const GameItem({super.key}); @override Widget build(BuildContext context) { return Container( padding: const EdgeInsets.symmetric( vertical: 15, horizontal: 20, ), decoration: BoxDecoration( color: Colors.white, borderRadius: BorderRadius.circular(10), ), child: Row( crossAxisAlignment: CrossAxisAlignment.start, children: [ Expanded( child: Row( children: [ Expanded( child: Column( crossAxisAlignment: CrossAxisAlignment.start, children: const [ Text( 'Boston Celtics', style: TextStyle( fontSize: 18, fontWeight: FontWeight.w400, ), ), SizedBox(height: 15), Text( 'Chicago Bulls', style: TextStyle( fontSize: 18, fontWeight: FontWeight.w400, ), ), ], ), ), const Expanded( child: Center( child: Text( '08:00 PM', style: TextStyle( fontSize: 18, fontWeight: FontWeight.w600, ), ), ), ), ], ), ), ], ), ); }
}

Create a testGoldens test

With a widget to test the appearance, becomes easy to write a test. Use the goldenTest() function from the alchemist package to define one test, this function allows you to define a widget to test the appearance and the file name to generate the Golden File.

goldenTest( 'displayed the basketball game', fileName: 'baskteball_game_widget', constraints: const BoxConstraints( maxWidth: 300, ), builder: () => const GameItem(),
);

Generating the Golden File

It's time to generate the Golden File after writing the test using the goldenTest() function. You can execute the command below to generate the Golden File.

flutter test --update-goldens

You may notice that the test directory has been updated with a new directory called goldens containing the Golden Files generated by the Golden Test, for example:

test/
    goldens/
        ci/
            - baskteball_game_widget.png
        macos/
            - baskteball_game_widget.png

The golden tests generate the macos/ subdirectory because while I'm writing this article I'm using a MacOS operation system. Whether you are using Linux or Windows your golden tests will generate the subdirectory dealing with your operating system with subtle differences between the platforms.

Golden Test in continuous integration workflow

You may notice that the goldens/ directory also has the ci/ subdirectory. The ci/ subdirectory is generated to deal with the subtle differences in rendering between MacOS, Linux, and Windows platforms.

Enabling the continuous integration using —dart-define

To enable continuous integration environment is necessary to disable the tests locally. We can use the IS_CONTINUOUS_INTEGRATION environment declaration to identify when the tests are running on the continuous integration environment and obtain this value using the bool.fromEnvironment.

Go back to the flutter_test_config.dart in the test/ directory and update the prepareGoldenTests to obtain the IS_CONTINUOUS_INTEGRATION declaration and disables the tests locally when this value is true.

void prepareGoldenTests(FutureOr<void> Function() testMain) { const isContinuosIntegration = bool.fromEnvironment('IS_CONTINUOUS_INTEGRATION'); return AlchemistConfig.runWithConfig( config: const AlchemistConfig( platformGoldensConfig: PlatformGoldensConfig( renderShadows: true, obscureText: false, enabled: isContinuosIntegration ), ), run: testMain, );
}

Running tests using the —dart-define

Running the tests using the —-dart-define environment declaration is pretty simple. See the command below to run the tests for a continuous integration environment.

flutter test --dart-define=IS_CONTINUOUS_INTEGRATION=true

Conclusion

The Golden Test is important for maintaining the code and the project's health. Sometimes, the standard Golden Test tool is insufficient to cover all cases possible; therefore, using the Alchemist tool gives developers a powerful tool to develop the appearance test and a way to use it in their continuous integration environments.
Items.Author: leonardorosaa

Items.Title: 如何设置您的 Raspberry Pi：WIFI、Docker 等新手指南！ | How to Set Up Your Raspberry Pi: A Beginner’s Guide to WIFI, Docker, and More!
Items.Description:
我最近在我的一个储物盒中找到了我的 Raspberry Pi 3B。 作为一款小巧、价格实惠且用途广泛的计算机，可用于从媒体中心到家庭自动化系统的广泛项目。

如果您是 Raspberry Pi 的新手，设置您的设备可能有点令人生畏，但不要担心 — 它比您想象的要容易。 在本指南中，我们将引导您完成启动和运行 Raspberry Pi 所需的步骤。

连接显示器并设置键盘

第一步是将显示器和键盘连接到 Raspberry Pi。 您需要一个带 HDMI 输入的显示器和一个 USB 键盘。 将 HDMI 线插入树莓派的 HDMI 端口，另一端插入显示器。 然后将 USB 键盘连接到 Raspberry Pi 上的 USB 端口之一。

首次启动 Raspberry Pi 时，系统会要求您选择键盘布局。 这很重要，因为它会影响键盘的行为方式。 确保为您的键盘选择正确的布局。 如果您不确定选择哪种布局，请尝试您所在地区的默认布局。

如何更改 Raspberry Pi 上的键盘布局？ 覆盆子秘诀

在 CLI 中设置 WIFI/LAN

接下来，您需要将 Raspberry Pi 连接到互联网。 您可以通过 GUI 执行此操作，方法是单击右上角的 WIFI 图标。 或者在 CLI 中更新它。

这可以手动更新，或者通过配置 CLI _raspi-config_ ，网络更改后可能需要重新启动。

首次启动后的任何时候，如果您想更改键盘布局、区域、WIFI 等，请在您的终端中运行sudo raspi-config

有关_raspi-config_的更多信息：

打开配置…_www.raspberrypi.com

安装 docker 和 docker compose

最后，您需要在 Raspberry Pi 上安装 Docker 和 Docker Compose。 要安装 Docker 和 Docker Compose，请按照以下教程中概述的步骤操作：

如何在树莓派上安装 Docker 和 Docker-Compose

首先..._dev.to

请注意，您可能不需要为 ARM 架构上最新版本的 Docker 安装 pip3 或 Python 3，Docker Compose 可能会默认安装。 您也可能不需要设置用户组。

就是这样！ 您现在应该拥有一个功能齐全的 Raspberry Pi，并安装了 Docker 和 Docker Compose。 从这里开始，您可以开始构建自己的项目并探索这台令人惊叹的小型计算机的全部潜力。 这些是一些很酷的家庭项目，可以在 Pi 上的 docker 中运行。



原文:

I recently found my Raspberry Pi 3B in one of my storage box. As a small, affordable and versatile computer that can be used for a wide range of projects, from media centers to home automation systems.

If you’re new to Raspberry Pi, setting up your device can be a little daunting, but don’t worry — it’s easier than you might think. In this guide, we’ll walk you through the steps required to get your Raspberry Pi up and running.

Connect Monitor and Set up Keyboard

The first step is to connect a monitor and a keyboard to your Raspberry Pi. You’ll need a monitor with an HDMI input, and a USB keyboard. Plug the HDMI cable into the HDMI port on the Raspberry Pi, and the other end into the monitor. Then connect the USB keyboard to one of the USB ports on the Raspberry Pi.

When you first boot up your Raspberry Pi, you’ll be asked to choose a keyboard layout. This is important, as it will affect the way your keyboard behaves. Make sure to choose the correct layout for your keyboard. If you’re unsure which layout to choose, try the default layout for your region.

How to Change your Keyboard Layout on Raspberry Pi? (RPI OS) | RaspberryTips

_If, like me, you don't use a QWERTY keyboard, the first thing you are probably looking for on your Raspberry Pi is how…_raspberrytips.com

Set up WIFI/LAN in CLI

Next, you’ll need to connect your Raspberry Pi to the internet. You can do this either via GUI, by clicking the WIFI icon at right corner. (If GUI Desktop Environment was installed)

Or update it in CLI. 

Raspberry Pi store the network config in /etc/wpa_supplicant/wpa_supplicant.conf 

This could be manually updated, or through the config CLI _raspi-config_ , a restart may be required after netwoek changed.

Anytime after the first booting, if you want to change keyboard layout, region, WIFI etc, run sudo raspi-config in your terminal

More about _raspi-config_:

Raspberry Pi Documentation - Configuration

_raspi-config is the Raspberry Pi configuration tool originally written by Alex Bradbury. To open the configuration…_www.raspberrypi.com

Install docker and docker compose

Finally, you’ll want to install Docker and Docker Compose on your Raspberry Pi. Docker is a tool for building, deploying, and managing containers, while Docker Compose is a tool for defining and running multi-container Docker applications. To install Docker and Docker Compose, follow the steps outlined in the following tutorial:

How To Install Docker and Docker-Compose On Raspberry Pi

_Raspberry Pi with a running Raspbian OS SSH connection enabled To do this you can check Raspberry Pi Setup. First of…_dev.to

Note that you may not need to install pip3 or Python 3 for the latest version of Docker on the ARM architecture, and Docker Compose may be installed by default. You also may not need to set up a user group.

That’s it! You should now have a fully functional Raspberry Pi with Docker and Docker Compose installed. From here, you can start building your own projects and exploring the full potential of this amazing little computer. These are some cool home projects could be run in docker on Pi.

Items.Title: 如何使用 VirtualBox 在 CentOS 7 上配置静态 IP 地址 | How to configure a static IP address on CentOS 7 with VirtualBox
Items.Description:
本文旨在说明如何使用 VirtualBox 在 CentOS 7 虚拟机上配置静态 IP 地址。

我们将一步步进行，在这篇博客的最后，您将能够使用一个永不改变的 IP ssh 进入您的虚拟机。

什么是静态IP？

每台计算机都有一个随机的本地 IP 地址，除非您指定了相反的地址。 这些地址不是固定的。 这意味着他们可以改变。

在大多数情况下，您不关心 IP 地址，但您通常会关心虚拟机。

如果您有一个在虚拟机上运行的 MySQL 服务，您可能希望保存一次连接配置并每次都重新使用它。 如果 IP 地址发生变化，您必须修改连接设置。

另一种方法是使用端口转发。 在您拥有 3 个或更多具有多个服务和端口需要跟踪的虚拟机之前，这种方法是可行的。

下图显示了具有静态 IP 地址的理想本地开发环境。

配置 VirtualBox 网络

在更改 VM Linux 配置之前，我们必须在 VirtualBox 应用程序中设置一些网络设置。

我们希望我们的 VM 具有以下内容：

  • 访问互联网
  • 访问我们的主机

而且我们还希望能够通过IP访问VM。

第 1 步：停止虚拟机

在执行以下步骤之前，您必须停止 VM。

第 2 步：创建以太网适配器

单击工具 - 网络并确保您已创建以太网适配器。 记下 IPv4 前缀，因为静态 IP 将在此范围内。

在上图中：

  • 网关是192.168.56.1
  • 网络掩码为 255.255.255.0（24 位）

我的 VM 的静态 IP 将在 192.168.56.xx 范围内。

第 3 步：更换适配器

右键单击虚拟机并选择“网络”选项卡。
我们将添加 2 个适配器：

  1. 第一个将是 NAT。 这样虚拟机就可以访问互联网了。
  2. 第二个必须是带有上一步以太网适配器的“Host-only Adapter”。 这是将分配静态 IP 的适配器。

配置虚拟机 Centos 7

现在我们已经配置了 VirtualBox 网络，我们将配置内部 VM 网络设置。

第一步：启动虚拟机

双击 VM 或右键单击并启动。

第二步：获取连接名称

我们知道静态 IP 将分配给第二个适配器，即 Host-only 适配器。

让我们使用ip addr | head -n 20检查 vm 网络环回

  • 现在我们知道我们必须为enp0s8设备分配一个 IP。 要获取其连接名称，您必须执行以下语句：

    nmcli -p device

    连接名称是“以太网连接 1”。

    第三步：配置连接IP

    有两种方法：

    • 使用nmtui图形化

    • 原文:

    This article aims to explain how to configure a static IP address on a CentOS 7 virtual machine using VirtualBox.

    We will go step by step, and at the end of this blog, you will be able to ssh into your virtual machine using a never-changing IP.

    What is a static IP?

    Every computer has a random local IP address unless you have specified the contrary. These addresses are not fixed. It means that they could change.

    In most cases, you don’t care about the IP address, but you usually do with virtual machines.

    If you have a MySQL service running on a virtual machine, you would want to save the connection configuration once and re-use it every time. If the IP address changes, you have to modify the connection settings.

    Another approach is to use port-forwarding. This approach is okay until you have 3 or more virtual machines with multiple services and ports to keep track of.

    The following image shows the ideal local development environment with static IP addresses.

    Configure VirtualBox Networking

    The app VirtualBox has some networking settings we have to set before changing the VM Linux configuration.

    We want our VM to have the following:

    • Access to the internet
    • Access to our host computer

    And we also want to be able to access the VM by IP.

    Step 1: Stop the VM

    You have to stop the VM before doing the following steps.

    Step 2: Create Ethernet Adapter

    Click on Tools - Networks and make sure you have an ethernet adapter created. Write down the IPv4 Prefix, because the static IP will be in this range.

    In the image above:

    • The gateway is 192.168.56.1
    • The network mask is 255.255.255.0 (24 bits)

    The static IP of my VM will be in the 192.168.56.xx range.

    Step 3: Change adapters

    Right-click on the virtual machine and choose the “Network” tab.
    We are going to add 2 adapters:

    1. The first one is going to be a NAT. This way the VM will have internet access.
    2. The second one has to be a “Host-only Adapter” with the ethernet adapter of the previous step. This is the adapter that will have the static IP assigned.

    Configure VM Centos 7

    Now that we have configured the VirtualBox networking, we will configure the inner VM networking settings.

    Step 1: Start the VM

    Double-click on the VM or right-click and start.

    Step 2: Get the connection name

    We know the static IP will be assigned to the second adapter, the Host-only Adapter.

    Let’s check the vm networking using ip addr | head -n 20:

    1. loopback
    2. enp0s3: NAT Adapter
    3. enp0s8: Host-Only Adapter

    Now we know we have to assign an IP to the enp0s8 device. To get its connection name, you have to execute the following statement:

    nmcli -p device

    The connection name is “Ethernet connection 1”.

    Step 3: Configure connection IP

    There are two ways of doing it:

    • Graphically with nmtui
    • With bash statements and nmcli

    In our case, we will execute some statements in the console.

    connection_name="Ethernet connection 1"
ip=192.168.56.2
gateway=192.168.56.1
bits=24

nmcli con mod "$connection_name" ipv4.addresses $ip/$bits
nmcli con mod "$connection_name" ipv4.gateway $gateway
nmcli con mod "$connection_name" ipv4.method manual
nmcli con up "$connection_name"

    If we execute ip addr | head -n 20 again, we will see the previous IP address.

    Step 4: Reboot

    The last step is to reboot the VM

    Test the connection

    Now we can connect to the VM using the command ssh user@192.168.56.2

Items.Title: 使用 ILLA Cloud 为公民开发者赋能 | Empowering Citizen Developers with ILLA Cloud
Items.Description:
公民开发人员是业务用户，他们可以在几乎没有编码知识的情况下创建自己的应用程序。 他们使用低代码和无代码平台等工具，使他们能够在没有编码经验的情况下构建应用程序。 公民开发不仅仅是构建应用程序。 它涉及遵循最佳实践和框架来实施公民发展项目，同时降低影子 IT 的风险。

但为什么企业需要公民开发者？ 公民发展的利弊和挑战是什么？ 在这篇博文中，我们将回答这些问题并为公民开发人员推荐一个软件堆栈，其中包括 ILLA Cloud，这是一个强大的开源低代码平台，供开发人员构建内部工具。

为什么企业需要公民开发者？

企业转向公民开发人员的原因有很多。 一些主要原因包括：

  • 快速周转：鼓励公民开发人员的最明显原因是在构建新解决方案时尽量减少延迟。 与积压时间长且资源有限的传统 IT 团队相比，公民开发人员可以更快地响应不断变化的业务需求和客户需求。
  • 商业意识：利用公民开发人员的另一个原因是弥合技术专长和商业意识之间的差距。 公民开发人员比可能不与最终用户或客户直接接触的外部供应商或 IT 团队更了解业务环境和目标。
  • 促进创新：公民发展还通过允许业务用户在不依赖 IT 批准或支持的情况下试验新想法和解决方案来促进创新。 公民开发人员可以测试不同的假设，快速迭代并从失败中吸取教训，而无需承担重大投资或中断的风险。
  • 管理影子 IT：影子 IT 是指组织内的员工使用未经授权或未经批准的软件或硬件。 影子 IT 绕过 IT 控制和策略，给企业带来安全、合规和运营风险。 通过为公民开发人员提供经批准的工具和平台，企业可以减少对影子 IT 的需求，并提高应用程序开发活动的可见性和问责制。
  • 消除孤岛：最后，公民发展可以通过支持不同团队和部门之间的跨职能协作和沟通来帮助消除组织内的孤岛。 公民开发者可以与其他用户分享他们的应用程序，征求反馈意见，采纳建议并根据用户需求改进他们的解决方案。

公民发展：利弊与挑战

公民发展对企业有很多好处，但也有一些缺点和挑战。 以下是公民发展的一些利弊和挑战：

公民发展的优点

  • 提高生产力：公民开发人员可以比传统 IT 团队更快、更便宜地构建应用程序，从而减少开发时间和成本。 他们还可以自动执行重复性任务和工作流程，从而腾出时间和资源用于更具战略意义的计划。
  • 提高用户满意度：公民开发人员可以创建适合其特定需求和偏好的应用程序，从而提高用户满意度和参与度。 他们还可以根据需要更新和修改他们的应用程序，确保他们满足不断变化的要求和期望。
  • 增强敏捷性：与传统 IT 团队相比，公民开发人员可以更快、更轻松地适应不断变化的市场条件和客户需求，从而增强业务敏捷性和竞争力。 他们还可以利用现有数据和系统，将它们集成到他们的应用程序中，而不会中断现有流程和操作。

公民发展的缺点

  • 缺乏标准化：公民开发人员可能会使用不同的工具和平台来创建他们的应用程序，从而导致整个组织缺乏标准化和一致性。 这可能会导致兼容性问题、重复工作和维护挑战。
  • 质量差：公民开发人员可能不具备必要的技能和知识来创建满足安全性、性能和可靠性标准的高质量应用程序。 他们还可能引入会损害其应用程序功能和可用性的错误和错误。
  • 有限的可扩展性：普通开发人员可能会创建适用于其特定用例的应用程序，但可能无法很好地扩展以适应更大或更复杂的场景。 他们还可能面临跨不同环境和设备部署和管理应用程序的挑战。

公民发展的挑战

  • 治理：公民发展的最大挑战之一是确保对应用程序开发活动进行适当的治理和监督。 企业需要为公民开发者制定明确的政策和指南，例如他们可以使用哪些工具和平台，他们可以访问哪些数据和系统，以及他们需要遵循哪些审批和测试流程。 他们还需要监控和审核他们的应用程序，确保它们符合安全、隐私和监管标准。
  • 教育：公民发展的另一个挑战是为公民开发人员提供充分的教育和培训，确保他们拥有创建有效和高效应用程序的技能和知识。 企业需要为公民开发者提供学习资源和支持，例如教程、文档、论坛和导师。

公民开发者的软件堆栈

为了赋予公民开发者权力，企业需要为他们提供一个软件堆栈，使他们能够轻松、安全地创建、部署和管理他们的应用程序。 软件堆栈是一组协同工作以提供特定功能或服务的软件组件。 公民开发人员的软件堆栈通常包括：

  • 核心平台：这是一个应用程序构建器，允许公民开发人员使用低代码或无代码工具创建应用程序。 核心平台应提供图形用户界面 (GUI)、拖放组件、预构建模板和集成，以简化应用程序开发过程。 核心平台还应提供安全性、可扩展性和治理功能，以确保符合 IT 标准和政策。
  • 相关工作流程：这些是公民开发人员在创建应用程序之前、期间和之后需要执行的流程和任务。 相关工作流程可能包括数据收集、分析、可视化、报告、自动化、协作和反馈。 相关的工作流程可能需要额外的工具和平台来补充核心平台并增强其功能。
  • 利益相关者的支持：这是公民开发人员需要从他们的经理、IT 团队和最终用户那里获得的批准和支持，才能成功实施他们的应用程序。 利益相关者的支持可能涉及展示其应用程序的价值和优势，解决其应用程序的问题和风险，并确保其应用程序与业务目标和战略保持一致和协调。

原文:

Citizen developers are business users who can create their own applications with little to no coding knowledge. They use tools like low code and no code platforms that enable them to build apps without prior coding experience. Citizen development is much more than just building an application. It involves following best practices and frameworks to implement citizen development projects while mitigating the risk of shadow IT.

But why do businesses need citizen developers? And what are the pros, cons and challenges of citizen development? In this blog post, we will answer these questions and recommend a software stack for citizen developers, featuring ILLA Cloud, a robust open source low code platform for developers to build internal tools.

Why do businesses need citizen developers?

There are several reasons why businesses are turning to citizen developers. Some of the main reasons include:

  • Quick turnarounds: The most obvious reason to encourage citizen developers is to minimize delays when it comes time to build new solutions. Citizen developers can respond faster to changing business needs and customer demands than traditional IT teams that have long backlogs and limited resources.
  • Commercial awareness: Another reason to leverage citizen developers is to bridge the gap between technical expertise and commercial awareness. Citizen developers understand the business context and goals better than external vendors or IT teams that may not have direct contact with end-users or customers.
  • Fostering innovation: Citizen development also fosters innovation by allowing business users to experiment with new ideas and solutions without depending on IT approval or support. Citizen developers can test different hypotheses, iterate quickly and learn from failures without risking major investments or disruptions.
  • Governing shadow IT: Shadow IT refers to the use of unauthorized or unapproved software or hardware by employees within an organization. Shadow IT poses security, compliance and operational risks for businesses as it bypasses IT controls and policies. By empowering citizen developers with approved tools and platforms, businesses can reduce the need for shadow IT and increase visibility and accountability of app development activities.
  • Eliminating silos: Finally, citizen development can help eliminate silos within an organization by enabling cross-functional collaboration and communication among different teams and departments. Citizen developers can share their apps with other users, solicit feedback, incorporate suggestions and improve their solutions based on user needs.

Citizen development: pros, cons & challenges

Citizen development has many benefits for businesses, but it also comes with some drawbacks and challenges. Here are some of the pros, cons and challenges of citizen development:

Pros of Citizen Development

  • Increased productivity: Citizen developers can build apps faster and cheaper than traditional IT teams, reducing development time and costs. They can also automate repetitive tasks and workflows, freeing up time and resources for more strategic initiatives.
  • Improved user satisfaction: Citizen developers can create apps that are tailored to their specific needs and preferences, improving user satisfaction and engagement. They can also update and modify their apps as needed, ensuring they meet changing requirements and expectations.
  • Enhanced agility: Citizen developers can adapt to changing market conditions and customer demands more quickly and easily than traditional IT teams, enhancing business agility and competitiveness. They can also leverage existing data and systems, integrating them into their apps without disrupting existing processes and operations.

Cons of Citizen Development

  • Lack of standardization: Citizen developers may use different tools and platforms to create their apps, resulting in a lack of standardization and consistency across the organization. This can lead to compatibility issues, duplication of efforts and maintenance challenges.
  • Poor quality: Citizen developers may not have the necessary skills and knowledge to create high-quality apps that meet security, performance and reliability standards. They may also introduce errors and bugs that compromise the functionality and usability of their apps.
  • Limited scalability: Citizen developers may create apps that work well for their specific use cases, but may not scale well for larger or more complex scenarios. They may also face challenges in deploying and managing their apps across different environments and devices.

Challenges of Citizen Development

  • Governance: One of the biggest challenges of citizen development is ensuring proper governance and oversight of app development activities. Businesses need to establish clear policies and guidelines for citizen developers, such as what tools and platforms they can use, what data and systems they can access, and what approval and testing processes they need to follow. They also need to monitor and audit their apps, ensuring they comply with security, privacy and regulatory standards.
  • Education: Another challenge of citizen development is providing adequate education and training for citizen developers, ensuring they have the skills and knowledge to create effective and efficient apps. Businesses need to offer learning resources and support for citizen developers, such as tutorials, documentation, forums and mentors.

Software stack for citizen developers

To empower citizen developers, businesses need to provide them with a software stack that enables them to create, deploy and manage their apps easily and securely. A software stack is a set of software components that work together to provide a specific functionality or service. A software stack for citizen developers typically consists of:

  • A core platform: This is the app-builder that allows citizen developers to create apps using low code or no code tools. A core platform should offer a graphical user interface (GUI), drag-and-drop components, pre-built templates and integrations that simplify the app development process. A core platform should also provide security, scalability and governance features that ensure compliance with IT standards and policies.
  • Related workflows: These are the processes and tasks that citizen developers need to perform before, during and after creating their apps. Related workflows may include data collection, analysis, visualization, reporting, automation, collaboration and feedback. Related workflows may require additional tools and platforms that complement the core platform and enhance its functionality.
  • Stakeholder buy-in: This is the approval and support that citizen developers need to obtain from their managers, IT teams and end-users to successfully implement their apps. Stakeholder buy-in may involve demonstrating the value and benefits of their apps, addressing the concerns and risks of their apps, and ensuring the alignment and coordination of their apps with the business goals and strategies.

ILLA Cloud

One of the best software stacks for citizen developers is ILLA Cloud, a robust open source low code platform for developers to build internal tools. ILLA Cloud offers many advantages for citizen developers, such as:

  • Easy to use: ILLA Cloud has a user-friendly interface that allows citizen developers to build apps using drag-and-drop components, pre-built templates and integrations. ILLA Cloud also has a library of components and actions that enable citizen developers to add features and functionality to their apps without writing code.
  • Flexible and customizable: ILLA Cloud allows citizen developers to customize their apps according to their specific needs and preferences. ILLA Cloud supports various data sources, such as databases, API/GraphQL endpoints, SaaS tools and cloud storage services. ILLA Cloud also allows citizen developers to modify the look and feel of their apps, as well as the logic and behavior of their apps.
  • Secure and scalable: ILLA Cloud ensures the security and scalability of the apps created by citizen developers. ILLA Cloud provides encryption, authentication and authorization features that protect the data and access of the apps. ILLA Cloud also provides cloud hosting and deployment features that ensure the performance and availability of the apps.
  • Open source and affordable: ILLA Cloud is an open source platform that is driven by the community and the feedback of the users. ILLA Cloud is free to use and offers various pricing plans for different needs and budgets. ILLA Cloud also provides documentation, tutorials, forums and mentors to help citizen developers learn and grow.

Conclusion

Citizen development is a powerful trend that is transforming the way businesses create and use applications. Citizen developers are business users who can create their own applications with little to no coding knowledge, using tools like low code and no code platforms.

Citizen development has many benefits for businesses, such as increasing productivity, improving user satisfaction, enhancing agility, governing shadow IT and eliminating silos. However, citizen development also has some drawbacks and challenges, such as lack of standardization, poor quality, limited scalability, governance and education.

To empower citizen developers, businesses need to provide them with a software stack that enables them to create, deploy and manage their apps easily and securely. One of the best software stacks for citizen developers is ILLA Cloud, a robust open source low code platform for developers to build internal tools. ILLA Cloud offers many advantages for citizen developers, such as easy to use, flexible and customizable, secure and scalable, open source and affordable.
